mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 6 Series 620d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 620d engine (B47D20B) typically costs £1,800–£3,000 supply only. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,400. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 6 Series 620d engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B47D20B is a significantly improved engine over the N47 – timing chain issues are less prevalent, but pre‑2018 units remain susceptible to tensioner wear. With regular oil changes (8,000–10,000 miles) and proper maintenance, the B47 can exceed 120,000 miles without major issues.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 6 Series 620d?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most common issues are B47 timing chain tensioner wear (pre‑2018 units), EGR valve/cooler failure, DPF blockage from urban driving, and oil leaks (valve cover gasket, oil filter housing gasket).

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a 620d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a tidy £10,000–£25,000 6 Series, a £1,800–£3,500 recon is 7–35% of the car's value – often worth it on well‑maintained examples. If the timing chain has snapped, a full engine replacement may be necessary. Get a full diagnostic assessment before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my 6 Series's engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 620d uses the B47D20B (also B47D20O1) 2.0‑litre twin‑turbo diesel. The engine code is stamped on the engine block and listed on your V5C. The 620d badge itself confirms the engine – it's the 190 PS diesel offered in the G32 6 Series Gran Turismo.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair a 620d engine issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most issues – timing chain tensioner wear (if caught early), EGR valve failure, DPF blockage, oil leaks – are repairable. However, if the timing chain has snapped, the engine is an interference design – pistons will strike valves. In this case, a full engine replacement is usually more cost‑effective than a rebuild.

problems.title: B47 Timing Chain Tensioner Wear (Pre‑2018 B47 Units)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise during cold starts, metallic ticking from the engine, engine warning light — left unaddressed, this can escalate into catastrophic chain failure.
problems.replacementCost: £1,800–£3,500 [PLACEHOLDER] (if chain snaps and damages the engine).

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear cold‑start rattle, book an inspection immediately.

problems.recommendation: While the B47 is significantly more robust than the N47, pre‑2018 B47 units remain susceptible to chain tensioner wear, leading to the characteristic "diesel rattle" on cold starts. Left unaddressed, this rattle can escalate into catastrophic chain failure, bent valves, or a seized engine. At the first sign of cold‑start rattle, have the timing system inspected immediately. B47 engines with regular oil changes and proper maintenance can exceed 120,000 miles without major issues.

problems.title: EGR Valve / Cooler Failure (B47)
problems.symptoms: Reduced power, rough idle, engine warning light, increased fuel consumption, black smoke from exhaust, failed emissions test.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if engine warning light appears.

problems.recommendation: EGR system failure is a common issue on B47 diesel engines. The EGR valve can become clogged with soot, and the EGR cooler can develop leaks, leading to coolant loss. Regular motorway driving and using high‑quality diesel can help reduce EGR clogging. If the engine warning light appears with EGR‑related codes, have the system diagnosed promptly.

problems.title: DPF Blockage (Urban Driving)
problems.symptoms: Reduced power, increased fuel consumption, engine warning light, DPF warning message, failed emissions test, oil level rising (fuel dilution).

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon if DPF warning appears.

problems.recommendation: The 620d's twin‑turbo diesel is sensitive to driving patterns. If used primarily for short urban trips, the Diesel Particulate Filter (DPF) will clog. Regular motorway runs (30+ minutes at 60+ mph) are essential to maintain the exhaust filtration system. Oil changes every 8,000–10,000 miles are recommended rather than BMW's suggested longer intervals to prolong engine and DPF life.

problems.title: Oil Leaks — Valve Cover & Oil Filter Housing (B47)
problems.symptoms: Oil spots under the vehicle, burning oil smell, low oil level warning, visible oil leak from the top or front of the engine.
problems.repairCost: £300–£800 [PLACEHOLDER] (valve cover gasket or oil filter housing gasket replacement).
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (if caught early; ignored oil loss can lead to engine damage).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if oil leak is detected.

problems.recommendation: The B47 is known for oil leaks from the valve cover gasket and oil filter housing gasket. If the oil filter housing gasket fails, oil can leak onto the serpentine belt, causing it to slip or break — which can then be ingested into the engine through the front crankshaft seal, causing catastrophic failure. If you notice oil spots under your 620d or a burning oil smell, investigate immediately.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW 6 Series 620d — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Catch it early — cheaper than engine replacement
Full engine replacement — valve damage is almost certain
Worth fixing — common and well‑understood repair
Full engine replacement often more cost‑effective

buyingChecks.title: Buying This BMW 6 Series 620d? Check These First
buyIf: * Full BMW service history with documented oil changes (every 8,000–10,000 miles)
* B47: Timing chain tensioner checked, especially on pre‑2018 units
* Evidence of regular motorway driving (DPF‑friendly usage)
* A well‑maintained example with lower mileage
avoidIf: * Any sign of cold‑start rattle — timing chain tensioner warning
* No evidence of regular oil changes — the B47 is intolerant of neglect
* DPF warnings on the dashboard — indicates urban‑only driving
* Poor service history — these engines demand strict maintenance

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The BMW 620d offers efficient, refined twin‑turbo diesel performance in the spacious Gran Turismo body. The B47D20B is a significantly improved engine over the N47 — timing chain issues are less prevalent, but pre‑2018 units remain susceptible to tensioner wear. Buy on service history above all else — a vehicle with documented timing chain maintenance and regular oil changes is a safe bet; a neglected example can be expensive to repair.

cta.label: → Compare 6 Series 620d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 6 Series 620d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: 1. Cold Start — Any rattle? (B47 timing chain tensioner warning — stop immediately)
2. Service History — Regular oil changes (8,000–10,000 miles) — this is non‑negotiable
3. Timing Chain History — B47: Has the chain tensioner been inspected?
4. DPF Health — Has the vehicle done regular longer runs? (short trips kill DPFs)
5. EGR System — Any engine warning lights? Any EGR‑related codes?
6. Oil Leaks — Check under the vehicle and around the valve cover and oil filter housing
7. Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 1,000 miles?
8. MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures or any advisory notes
